switchGuard: Keep Objective-C/AppKit as the shipping app until every phase below is complete and the parity matrix has zero required missing features.

phases.title: Settings contract and settings shell
exitEvidence: SwiftUI reads and writes the same per-key UserDefaults contract
seconds-level time fields preserve the 10-second debug rhythm
overview, eye, stand, display, automation, and stats settings pages are represented
phases.recommendedNextPort: Port the settings contract first, then rebuild the overview/settings shell without replacing the shipping app.

phases.title: Dual timers and rest overlay policy
exitEvidence: eye and stand rest states reschedule independently
ordinary rest windows yield while topmost mode remains opt-in
phases.recommendedNextPort: Port independent timers, rest state transitions, yielded overlay behavior, and topmost policy.

phases.title: Automation, presentation, calendar, and display recovery
exitEvidence: automation priority remains ignore > auto-pause > notify-only
calendar, presentation, quiet-hours, and app policies explain their final action
display and settings-window recovery diagnostics remain available
phases.recommendedNextPort: Port policy explanation, keyword templates, EventKit checks, presentation detection, quiet hours, and display recovery diagnostics.

phases.title: Stats, backup, update, and support flow
exitEvidence: stats history, CSV export, JSON backup, and JSON restore preserve the current schema
about, update check, install guide, issue bundle, and support bundle remain available
release readiness and evidence capture still pass without full-screen smoke tests
phases.recommendedNextPort: Port stats schema, import/export, GitHub release lookup, install guide, issue bundle, and evidence hooks.
